vulnerability.title: Disable ATM Support
vulnerability.description: The Asynchronous Transfer Mode (ATM) is a protocol operating on network, data link, and physical layers, based on virtual circuits and virtual paths. To configure the system to prevent the atm kernel module from being loaded, add the following line to the file /etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf: install atm /bin/false This entry will cause a non-zero return value during a atm module installation and additionally convey the meaning of the entry to the user in form of an error message. If you would like to omit a non-zero return value and an error message, you may want to add a different line instead (both /bin/true and /bin/false are allowed by OVAL and will be accepted by the scan): install atm /bin/true
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if LC_ALL=C grep -q -m 1 "^install atm" /etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf ; then sed -i 's#^install atm.*#install atm /bin/false#g' /etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf else echo -e "\n# Disable per security requirements" >> /etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf echo "install atm /bin/false" >> /etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf fi if ! LC_ALL=C grep -q -m 1 "^blacklist atm$" /etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf ; then echo "blacklist atm" >> /etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation adds a line to /etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf to prevent the atm kernel module from being loaded. This is a non-disruptive change; ATM is not commonly used and disabling it does not affect core system functionality or access.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; Potential loss of ATM networking functionality if ATM is in use; Modprobe config changes may be overwritten by package updates

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for kernel_module_atm_disabled (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_kernel_module_atm_disabled). Could not verify.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to produce a plan to remediate the finding: disable ATM support. The remediation: add a line to /etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf: install atm /bin/false. This entry will cause a non-zero return value during a atm module installation and convey error message.

We need to output JSON with plan description and list of commands. Likely just one command: write_file or maybe using echo? But we have only run_cmd, read_file, write_file. To add a line to a file, we can use write_file with content being the line and path the file. Mode likely "append"? The write_file function description: "Write content to a single file on the target host." Probably we need to specify mode? Not given in parameters; maybe default is overwrite? We need to append, but we don't have mode parameter? Actually write_file parameters: path, content, mode. Yes there is mode. So we need to specify mode: "append". Provide content: "install atm /bin/false". Path: "/etc/modprobe.d/atm.conf". That will write file (overwrite?) but with mode append should add line.

We also might need to ensure the file exists? Not needed.

Thus plan: one command write_file.

Return JSON.
